lollmsVsCoder.responseProfiles.description: User-defined response modes that combine system prompts, thinking styles, and prefixes.

default.name: Balanced (Default)
default.description: Natural technical flow: Brief explanation followed by implementation.
default.systemPrompt:
### RESPONSE STYLE: BALANCED
- **Logic**: Briefly explain the technical approach or reasoning behind the solution first.
- **Implementation**: Provide the code or perform the actions immediately after the explanation.
- **Tone**: Professional, helpful, and direct.

default.name: Structured (Analytical)
default.systemPrompt:
### RESPONSE STYLE: STRUCTURED
- **MANDATORY LAYOUT**: You MUST follow this three-part structure for every response:
  1. **Observe**: Identify what is being asked or what issue was found in the context.
  2. **Think**: Describe the technical path chosen to resolve it and why.
  3. **Act**: Provide the actual implementation, code, or tool call.

default.name: Senior Coder
default.description: Focus on best practices, performance, and clean naming.
default.systemPrompt:
### RESPONSE STYLE: SENIOR CODER
- **Quality**: Prioritize DRY, KISS, and SOLID principles.
- **Performance**: Optimize for execution speed and memory efficiency.
- **Naming**: Use highly descriptive variable and function names.
- **Notes**: Briefly mention which best practices were applied.

default.name: Architect
default.description: Focus on high-level design patterns and maintainability.
default.systemPrompt:
### RESPONSE STYLE: ARCHITECT
- **Design**: Think in terms of components, scalability, and interfaces.
- **Patterns**: Suggest design patterns (e.g. Factory, Observer) where applicable.
- **Trade-offs**: Explain why one technical choice was made over another.

default.name: Minimalist
default.systemPrompt:
### RESPONSE STYLE: MINIMALIST
- **Directness**: Do not include introductions, conclusions, or 'Here is your code'.
- **Content**: Provide only the requested code block or the direct answer to the question.
- **Brevity**: Extreme conciseness.

default.name: Pedagogical (Teacher)
default.systemPrompt:
### RESPONSE STYLE: PEDAGOGICAL
- **Teaching**: Act as a mentor. Explain concepts deeply so the user learns the 'how' and 'why'.
- **Breakdown**: Use analogies and step-by-step walkthroughs.
- **Resources**: Suggest areas for further study if relevant.

default.name: Fast / No Think
default.systemPrompt: Bypass internal reasoning steps to provide the fastest possible response.

lollmsVsCoder.documentationMode.description: Automatically update project documentation (README, docs, etc.) after code modifications.

lollmsVsCoder.gitAutoWorkflow.description: Automatically create branches and prepare commits for agentic missions.

lollmsVsCoder.connectionProfiles.description: Saved connection environments (URL, Key, Model, etc.) for quick switching.

lollmsVsCoder.defaultResponseProfileId.description: The ID of the default response profile to use for new discussions.

lollmsVsCoder.remote.server.port.description: Port for the local webhook server (for Push notifications).

lollmsVsCoder.remote.slack.signingSecret.description: Slack Signing Secret (Required for Push/Events API).

lollmsVsCoder.remote.allowedUsers.description: List of User IDs allowed to interact with the bot. Leave empty to allow NO ONE (safe default).

lollmsVsCoder.remote.adminUsers.description: List of User IDs allowed to execute dangerous tools (File I/O, Terminal).

lollmsVsCoder.remote.allowedChannels.description: List of Channel IDs where the bot is allowed to reply.

lollmsVsCoder.moltbook.enable.description: Enable automated social interaction with Moltbook.com. WARNING: Enabling this allows the AI to post and interact on your behalf.

lollmsVsCoder.moltbook.apiKey.description: API Key for Moltbook. If empty, the extension will also check for MOLTBOOK_API_KEY in .env or system environment.

lollmsVsCoder.moltbook.botName.description: The display name for your AI Bot on Moltbook.

lollmsVsCoder.moltbook.botPurpose.default: An autonomous software engineering assistant integrated with VS Code.
lollmsVsCoder.moltbook.botPurpose.description: A brief description of your bot's purpose on the Moltbook network.

lollmsVsCoder.agent.permissions.description: Global permissions for the AI Agent tools.

shellExecution.description: Allow executing terminal commands and scripts.

filesystemWrite.description: Allow creating or updating files in the workspace.

filesystemRead.description: Allow reading file contents and listing directories.

internetAccess.description: Allow web search and website scraping.

screenCapture.description: Allow the agent to take screenshots of your desktop. (Privacy Sensitive)

webTesting.description: Allow the agent to browse and screenshot web pages/local servers using a headless browser.

lollmsVsCoder.agent.useRLM.description: Enable Recursive Language Model (RLM) mode for agents. This allows the agent to execute python code recursively to solve problems.

lollmsVsCoder.agent.maxSimultaneousAgent.description: Maximum number of agent tasks that can be executed in parallel.

lollmsVsCoder.agent.maxSteps.description: Maximum number of turns an agent can take before a Mission Timeout. High values are better for long tasks like training.

lollmsVsCoder.agent.executionPolicy.description: Code Execution Policy for the Agent.
enumDescriptions: Agent is forbidden from running code or shell commands.
Agent must wait for a user button click before running code (Default).
Agent can run code and shell commands autonomously.

lollmsVsCoder.agent.activeProfile.description: The default Mission Profile for the AI Agent.

lollmsVsCoder.agent.autoProfileSwitch.description: If enabled, the agent will analyze your objective at the start of a mission and automatically switch to a more suitable profile if one is found.

lollmsVsCoder.agentProfiles.description: Customizable mission protocols for the Agent Architect.

default.name: Software Architect (General)
default.description: General-purpose engineering, refactoring, and feature building.
default.protocol:
### 🏗️ MISSION PROTOCOL: SOFTWARE ARCHITECT
1. **ANALYSIS**: Map out dependencies before touching any code.
2. **MODULARITY**: Prefer small, testable modules.
3. **VERIFICATION**: Always run a build or test command after implementation.

default.name: Game Builder
default.description: Expert in Pygame, Godot, and Unity.
default.protocol:
### 🎮 MISSION PROTOCOL: GAME BUILDER
1. **STACK DETECTION**: Identify engine (Pygame, Godot, Unity).
2. **ASSET PIPELINE**: Use 'generate_image' and 'create_svg_asset'.
3. **LOGIC**: Build the game loop and state management.
4. **VISUAL TEST**: Use 'capture_desktop' to verify visual state.

default.name: Pentester (CVE Builder)
default.description: Hunts for vulnerabilities and builds documented CVE reports.
default.protocol:
### 🛡️ MISSION PROTOCOL: PENTESTER (CVE BUILDER)
1. **RECON**: Find dangerous sinks (eval, SQLi).
2. **EXPLOIT**: Write a reproduction script to prove the flaw.
3. **REPORT**: Use CVE Builder tool.
4. **PATCH**: Apply fix and verify it's blocked.

lollmsVsCoder.agent.envActivationScript.description: Command to run before every shell task (e.g., 'C:\Path\To\vcvarsall.bat x64'). Useful for MSVC or specific toolchains.

lollmsVsCoder.agent.createBranchOnEdit.description: Automatically create a new git branch (e.g., ai-task-123) before the Agent modifies files to prevent breaking existing code.

lollmsVsCoder.searchApiKey.description: API Key for the search provider (e.g., Google Custom Search API Key).

lollmsVsCoder.searchCx.description: Search Engine ID (CX) for Google Custom Search.

lollmsVsCoder.autoGenerateTitle.description: Automatically generate a title using AI when creating a new discussion from clipboard.

lollmsVsCoder.clipboardInsertRole.description: Role to use when pasting clipboard content into a new discussion.

lollmsVsCoder.useLollmsExtensions.description: Enable specific Lollms features (like server-side tokenization and context size) if the backend supports them. Defaults to true for Lollms backend.

lollmsVsCoder.ttiModelName.description: The model used for Text-to-Image (TTI) generation. Leave empty to let the server autoselect.

lollmsVsCoder.architectModelName.description: The 'Big' model used for planning and reasoning in Multi-Agent mode. Leave empty to use the Chat Model.

lollmsVsCoder.titlingModelName.description: Model used for generating discussion titles. Leave empty to use the Chat Model.

lollmsVsCoder.gitCommitModelName.description: Model used for generating git commit messages. Leave empty to use the Chat Model.

lollmsVsCoder.surgicalModelName.description: Model used for surgical code repairs and modifications. Leave empty to use the Chat Model.

lollmsVsCoder.summarizationModelName.description: Model used for long file processing and summarization. Leave empty to use the Chat Model.

lollmsVsCoder.verifyAndCorrectCodeBlocks.description: Automatically verify AI-generated diff/search blocks against files and attempt self-correction if they fail to apply.

lollmsVsCoder.chatPersona.default: You are Lollms, an expert AI software engineer. You always start your responses with a clear pedagogical breakdown of the **Observe**, **Think**, and **Act** phases before providing code or executing tools.

lollmsVsCoder.agentPersona.default: You are a specialized AI agent. Before performing tasks, you briefly summarize the **Observe** (context/issue), **Think** (approach), and **Act** (plan).

lollmsVsCoder.codeInspectorPersona.default: You are a senior code inspector AI. Your task is to analyze the provided code snippet for errors, bugs, vulnerabilities, and malicious content. You must be extremely critical and thorough.

lollmsVsCoder.commitMessagePersona.default: You are an expert AI assistant that writes conventional git commit messages.

lollmsVsCoder.failsafeContextSize.description: Manual Context Size Override. If your model uses more/less than detected, set this value (in tokens) to force the limit and remove the '(Approx)' label. Set to 0 to use auto-detection.

lollmsVsCoder.mcpServers.description: Configuration for MCP Servers. Support both local commands and remote SSE URLs.

lollmsVsCoder.companion.enableWebSearch.description: Allow the Companion (Quick Edit) to use Web Search.

lollmsVsCoder.companion.enableArxivSearc.description: Allow the Companion (Quick Edit) to use ArXiv Search.

lollmsVsCoder.userInfo.name.description: Your full name. Used by the AI for file headers and personalization.

lollmsVsCoder.userInfo.email.description: Your email address. Used for file headers.

lollmsVsCoder.userInfo.license.description: Default license to use in generated code headers.

lollmsVsCoder.userInfo.codingStyle.description: Brief description of your preferred coding style (e.g., 'Functional', 'PEP8', 'Concise').

lollmsVsCoder.herdParticipants.description: Deprecated: Use Pre-Answer and Post-Answer participants lists instead.

lollmsVsCoder.herdPreAnswerParticipants.description: List of models and personalities for Pre-Answer Brainstorming (Phase 1).

lollmsVsCoder.herdPostAnswerParticipants.description: List of models and personalities for Post-Answer Brainstorming (Phase 3).

lollmsVsCoder.herdRounds.description: Default number of rounds for Herd Mode brainstorming.

lollmsVsCoder.herdDynamicMode.description: Enable Dynamic Herd Mode: The AI generates participants automatically based on the prompt.

lollmsVsCoder.herdDynamicModelPool.description: Pool of available models for Dynamic Herd assignment. Each entry is { model: string, description: string }.

lollmsVsCoder.git.deleteBranchAfterMerge.description: Automatically delete the temporary feature branch after merging it into the original branch.

lollmsVsCoder.git.unstagedChangesBehavio.description: Action to take if unstaged changes exist when creating a new AI branch.
enumDescriptions: Stash changes and pop them after switching (if possible) or keep stashed.
Keep changes and carry them over to the new branch.

lollmsVsCoder.systemEnv.showOs.description: Automatically share your Operating System name with the AI in the system prompt.

lollmsVsCoder.systemEnv.showIp.description: Automatically share your local IP addresses with the AI (Useful for network-related tasks).

lollmsVsCoder.systemEnv.showShells.description: Automatically detect and share available shells (powershell, bash, etc.) with the AI.

lollmsVsCoder.systemEnv.customInfo.description: Provide additional environment context for the AI (e.g. 'I am using Git Bash on Windows', 'My Python version is 3.12').

lollmsVsCoder.distillWebResults.description: When scraping URLs, use the AI to distill/refactor the content based on your prompt before adding it to context.

lollmsVsCoder.thinkingMode.description: Enable Physical Thinking/Reasoning mode for compatible models (OpenAI o1/o3, Claude 3.7, DeepSeek R1, Ollama).

lollmsVsCoder.reasoningEffort.description: Computational depth for OpenAI o1/o3 models.

lollmsVsCoder.thinkingBudget.description: Token budget for Claude 3.7 Sonnet thinking mode.

lollmsVsCoder.testMode.description: Automatically generate unit tests after the AI modifies or generates code.

lollmsVsCoder.antiPromptInjection.description: Scans and removes potential hidden prompt injections from scraped web content.

lollmsVsCoder.searchInCacheFirst.description: When doing a web search, check the .lollms cache first to avoid redownloading known information.

lollmsVsCoder.graph.zoomSensitivity.description: Controls how fast the Architecture Graph zooms in/out.

lollmsVsCoder.graph.panningEnabled.description: Enable or disable dragging/panning the graph.

lollmsVsCoder.graph.zoomToCursor.description: If enabled, zooming will center on the mouse cursor position.

lollmsVsCoder.memory.autoDream.description: If enabled, Lollms will perform a Dream Cycle automatically at a specific time every day.

lollmsVsCoder.memory.dreamSchedule.description: The time (HH:MM) to perform the automatic Dream Cycle. (e.g., '01:00' for 1 AM).

lollmsVsCoder.developer.debugTools.description: Enable internal debugger UI to test tools and export diagnostic logs.

scripts.compile: npm run clean && npm run copy-assets && npm run build-webview && esbuild ./src/extension.ts --bundle --outfile=out/extension.js --external:vscode --format=cjs --platform=node --target=es2020 --sourcemap
scripts.watch: npm run clean && npm run copy-assets && npm run build-webview && esbuild ./src/extension.ts --bundle --outfile=out/extension.js --external:vscode --format=cjs --platform=node --target=es2020 --sourcemap --watch
scripts.esbuild-package: npm run clean && npm run copy-assets && npm run minify-webview && esbuild ./src/extension.ts --bundle --outfile=out/extension.js --external:vscode --format=cjs --platform=node --target=es2020 --minify
scripts.copy-assets: cpx "src/commands/chatPanel/webview/*.css" out/webview && cpx "media/**" out/media && cpx "node_modules/@vscode/codicons/dist/codicon.{css,ttf}" out/styles && cpx "node_modules/prismjs/themes/prism-tomorrow.css" out/styles && cpx "src/skills/**/*" out/skills && cpx "l10n/*.json" out/l10n && cpx "l10n/*.json" l10n && cpx "package.nls*.json" out
scripts.audit-fix: npm audit fix --audit-level=moderate || echo 'Audit fix finished with remaining issues.'
scripts.build-and-package: npm run clean && npm run audit-fix && npm run vscode:prepublish && npx @vscode/vsce package
